title: SubQuaia
author: Diana C. Zapata-Zuluaga
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
SubQuaia  is a subcatalog derived from the original  Quaia catalog , obtained after a data cleaning process designed to filter out sources with catastrophic relative errors in their spectrophotometric redshifts. The procedure used to construct this catalog is described in detail in the associated  thesis work . 
 As in the original catalog,  SubQuaia  includes files corresponding to two versions of the catalog, each with a different magnitude limit: one sample with G < 20.5 and another with G < 20.0. In this cleaned version, the G < 20.5 sample contains 793,862 sources (compared to 1,295,502 in the original catalog), while the G < 20.0 sample contains 507,868 sources (compared to 755,850 in the original). 
 The files provided are: 
 
 
  subquaiaG20.5.fits   
 
 
  subquaiaG20.0.fits  
 
 
 This dataset also provides  Volume-Limited Samples (VLS)  derived from the  SubQuaia  quasar catalog, for samples of G < 20.5 and G < 20.0. 
 Each VLS is defined by an absolute magnitude cut in the  i  band, from  Mᵢ = −21  to  Mᵢ = −29  in steps of 1 magnitude, resulting in nine subsets per sample. 
 
 
  VLS_G20.5.zip : contains  VLS_mag_lim_-21.fits  …  VLS_mag_lim_-29.fits  (G < 20.5). 
 
 
  VLS_G20.0.zip : contains  VLS_mag_lim_-21.fits  …  VLS_mag_lim_-29.fits  (G < 20.0).
